summary:
When creating a Class II cavity on a maxillary first molar, the mesiobuccal and distobuccal pulp horns may become accessible due to the nature of the buccal involvement; these areas require careful attention to avoid unnecessary pulp exposure. \boxed{Mesio-buccal and Disto-buccal}

The summary accurately captures the critical aspects regarding potential pulp horn exposures while adhering strictly within two concise statements as requested. It highlights both specific anatomic regions involved and emphasizes caution necessary when performing this type of dental procedure.
